summary: Full audit of P1/P2 fixes. All static checks pass. One failing requirement: act() warnings in 2 test files must be eliminated.

issues_found.description: React act() warnings in two test files. Tests pass but violate the zero-warnings requirement.

source_1.cause: PinPad.tsx calls setDigits([]) inside a 100ms setTimeout. Tests await that timeout with raw `await new Promise(setTimeout)` which is not wrapped in act(), so React flags the state update.

source_2.cause: ParentPage useEffect calls getSetting (mocked Promise), then calls setPinHash on resolution. The async resolution happens outside any act() boundary in the tests.

files_to_modify.change: Add `act` to testing-library imports. Wrap every `await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 200))` in `await act(async () => { ... })`.

Add `act` to testing-library imports. After each `render(<ParentPage />)` call, add `await act(async () => {})` to flush the useEffect's async getSetting → setPinHash chain. Wrap every `await new Promise(setTimeout)` in `await act(async () => { ... })`. Change the first synchronous test to async.

steps.action: Fix PinPad.test.tsx: import act, wrap setTimeout awaits in act()
Fix ParentPin.test.tsx: import act, add act flush after render, wrap setTimeout awaits in act()
Run npm test to confirm zero act() warnings and all 207 tests still pass
Run npx tsc --noEmit to confirm no TypeScript errors introduced
Write integration-report.md with PASS/FAIL for all 15 checks

risk_areas.risk: Wrapping render in act() may change timing of assertions in ParentPin tests
risk_areas.mitigation: Keep all existing waitFor() assertions; only add the act flush after render to allow async effects to complete before the test proceeds.

risk_areas.risk: act(async () => {}) may not flush all microtasks in all environments
risk_areas.mitigation: Keep the existing `await waitFor(() => expect(mockGetSetting).toHaveBeenCalled())` calls as a fallback assertion guard.

persistence_strategy: N/A — this task modifies test files only; no data persistence concerns
error_handling_approach: N/A — test-only changes; production error handling already verified and passing
assumptions: The only failing requirement is the act() warnings; all 13 static checks are already green.
Fixing tests (not source code) is the correct approach since the source code behavior is correct.
act(async () => {}) flushes microtasks in jsdom/Jest environment as documented by React Testing Library.
